articles.title: 2026 LiveLaw (SC) 541 | State of Himachal Pradesh & Ors. v. M/s Kundlas Loh Udyog
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled in State of Himachal Pradesh & Ors. v. M/s Kundlas Loh Udyog that promissory estoppel cannot be invoked for benefits that were never intended to be granted. The judgment clarifies the scope of this legal doctrine, which prevents the government from going back on promises made to citizens or entities. The case involved a dispute over alleged promises made by state authorities regarding tax exemptions or benefits.

articles.title: CfP: Indian Journal of Law and Legal Research [Vol 8 Issue 2, ISSN: 2582-8878, PIF: 7.14], Manupatra, HeinOnline & Google Scholar Indexed, Available in Libraries of European Parliament, Harvard, Stanford, Oxford, LSE & California Univ, Free DOI, Certificate of Excellence, Internships, Hard Copy: Submit by May 29
articles.summary: About the Journal IJLLR – Indian Journal of Law and Legal Research, ISSN: 2582-8878, is an online bi-monthly journal. articles.title: Peremptory Decrees In A Suit For Specific Performance Of A Contract
articles.summary: The article explains the legal concept of 'peremptory decrees' in the context of specific performance suits in India. A peremptory decree is a court order that, if not strictly complied with, can nullify the rights under the decree and strip the court of jurisdiction. This legal principle is relevant to contract enforcement and judicial authority in India.
articles.raw_summary: The word “peremptory” is defined as “ putting an end to or precluding a right of action, debate, or delay ”. In the field of law, when a peremptory/conditional order or decree is passed by a court of law, its effect is that if the conditions provided under the decree or order is not complied with strictly, the rights under the decree or order will get effaced. As a consequence, the court will not have any jurisdiction over the decree or order. The genesis of this article is in the question formu

articles.summary: In a dowry death case from Bhopal, the husband withdrew his anticipatory bail and agreed to surrender. The Madhya Pradesh High Court issued a notice to the mother-in-law after noting the State's contention that the anticipatory bail was granted hastily. The case involves a death reported on May 12, with the post-mortem conducted on May 14 and bail granted the next day. The court highlighted procedural irregularities in the bail grant.
articles.raw_summary: The Court also took note of the State s contention that the anticipatory bail was granted in a very hurried manner, as the death occurred on 12 May at night, the post-mortem was conducted on 14 May, the anticipatory bail application was filed on 14 May, and the same was allowed on 15 May. articles.title: Participation in Reassessment Proceedings Cannot Cure Section 143(2) Notice Absence: ITAT Nagpur
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Nagpur ruled that reassessment proceedings for tax purposes cannot proceed without the mandatory issuance of a notice under Section 143(2) of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal emphasized that even if the assessee participates in the proceedings, the absence of this statutory notice invalidates the entire process. This decision underscores the importance of procedural compliance in tax reassessments.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Nagpur held that reassessment proceedings cannot survive without issuance of a mandatory notice under Section 143(2). articles.title: No More Jumping the Queue: Cinematograph (Certification) Amendment Rules, 2026 Scrap Priority Scheme for Film Certification
articles.summary: The Indian government has amended the Cinematograph (Certification) Rules, 2026 to scrap the priority scheme for film certification. articles.title: ITAT Orders Recalculation of Section 270A Penalty Due to Wrong Under-Reported Income Computation
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has directed the recalculation of a penalty under Section 270A of the Income Tax Act after finding that the Assessing Officer (AO) incorrectly computed under-reported income. The ITAT ruled that under-reported income must be calculated as the difference between assessed income and income processed under Section 143(1)(a). The penalty computation has been sent back for recomputation due to the AO's flawed methodology.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that under-reported income must be calculated as the difference between assessed income and income processed under Section 143(1)(a). articles.title: Custom Duty Refund Claim Not Maintainable Without Challenging Assessment Order: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that importers cannot claim customs duty refunds without first challenging the assessment order under the amended Customs Act. The self-assessment process introduced by the Act is considered an assessment order, and refund claims must follow statutory remedies. This decision impacts how businesses handle duty refunds and compliance with customs regulations in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that self-assessment introduced under the amended Customs Act qualifies as an assessment order under Section 2(2). Importers seeking refund must first challenge such assessment through statutory remedies before filing refund claims. This ruling clarifies tax obligations for companies engaging foreign directors in export-related activities.

articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Chandigarh held that reassessment proceedings initiated under an incorrect and obsolete PAN suffered from a jurisdictional defect. articles.title: No Penalty Under GST With Out Liability
articles.summary: The article explains that under India's GST Act, penalties can only be imposed if there is a tax liability, contravention of rules, or deliberate intent to evade tax. Technical or clerical errors without intent to evade tax do not automatically trigger penalties. The analysis is based on legal provisions and aims to clarify enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The article explains that GST penalties generally require existence of tax liability, contravention, or deliberate intent to evade tax. Mere technical or clerical lapses without mens rea cannot automatically trigger penal action under the GST Act. The post No Penalty Under GST With Out Liability appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Innocent Recipient’s Burden: GST Challenges When Suppliers Turn “Fake”
articles.summary: Courts have ruled that genuine buyers who purchased goods or services with proper invoices and banking records cannot lose their Input Tax Credit (ITC) even if their suppliers later failed to deposit GST or became non-compliant. This protects innocent recipients from penalties due to supplier misconduct. The ruling reinforces the legal safeguards for bona fide transactions under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: Courts have repeatedly held that bona fide purchasers cannot lose Input Tax Credit merely because suppliers failed to deposit GST or later became non-compliant. Genuine transactions supported by invoices and banking records remain protected. The post Innocent Recipient s Burden: GST Challenges When Suppliers Turn  Fake  appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Indian government is pushing ministries to speed up the closure of unprofitable state-owned companies. Progress has been slow since a policy was approved eight years ago, with only one company successfully shut down. The move aims to address delays in both disinvestment and shutdown processes, which have raised concerns over inefficiency in public sector management.

articles.title: RBI unlikely to hike rates soon despite inflation risk, rate action only in Dec if needed: Icra's Aditi Nayar
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to hold off on interest rate hikes. Inflation risks are rising due to fuel prices and monsoon uncertainty. This cautious approach aims to balance economic growth and price stability.

articles.summary: Pranjul Bhandari, Chief India Economist at HSBC, believes India’s biggest challenge currently is pressure on its external balances. articles.title: Ebola screening begins at Visakhapatnam and Vijayawada airports; no case found so far
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government has initiated Ebola screening for travelers with recent African travel history at Visakhapatnam and Vijayawada airports, as well as the Visakhapatnam port. A 30-bed isolation ward has been prepared for any suspected cases. No Ebola cases have been detected so far. The move is part of proactive health surveillance measures.

articles.title: Congress national youth wing secretary Srinidhi resigns after AIADMK MLAs join TVK
articles.summary: The Congress party's national youth wing secretary resigned following the decision of its post-poll ally, TVK, to induct three AIADMK MLAs who had left the NDA. The move has raised concerns within the Congress about the consistency of its political principles, as it had previously stated it would not align with divisive forces.

articles.title: S. 307 IPC | Gravity Of Injury Not Enough To Convict For Attempt To Murder Without Proof Of Intention To Cause Death: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that conviction for attempt to murder under Section 307 of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) cannot be based solely on the severity of injuries sustained by the victim. The bench, comprising Justice Sanjay Karol and Justice Nongmeikapam Kotiswar Singh, emphasized that the prosecution must prove the accused's intention to cause death. The court stated: “…the gravity of the injury by itself cannot be determinative of the offence under Section 307 IPC unless the prosecution is abl
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has observed that unless the mens rea to cause death is proved, the conviction for attempt to commit murder cannot be sustained merely on account of the gravity of the injury suffered by the injured. “…the gravity of the injury by itself cannot be determinative of the offence under Section 307 IPC unless the prosecution is able to establish the requisite mens rea contemplated under the provision.”, observed a bench of Justice Sanjay Karol and Justice Nongmeikapam Kotiswar Singh

articles.title: Section 54 Exemption Cannot Be Denied Due to Joint Ownership With Spouse: ITAT Chandigarh
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Chandigarh ruled that joint ownership with a spouse cannot disqualify an individual from claiming tax exemption under Section 54 of the Income Tax Act. The decision was based on the fact that the entire investment for the new residential property came from the assessee (taxpayer). This ruling clarifies the eligibility criteria for Section 54 deductions, ensuring fairness in tax exemptions for joint property owners.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that joint ownership alone cannot restrict Section 54 deduction where the entire source of investment for the new residential property originated from the assessee. The post Section 54 Exemption Cannot Be Denied Due to Joint Ownership With Spouse: ITAT Chandigarh appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Rose Water Taxable at 18% GST Because It Was Not Recognised as Puja Samagri
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that rose water (Pooja Panneer) is taxable at 18% GST because it was not included in the exhaustive list of exempted puja samagri items. The exemption notification specified eligible items, and the word 'namely' made the list exhaustive. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of certain religious items under GST.
articles.raw_summary: The Tamil Nadu AAR held that “Pooja Panneer” could not claim GST exemption as puja samagri because the exemption notification specifically listed eligible items. The Authority ruled that the word “namely” made the list exhaustive and not illustrative. The post Rose Water Taxable at 18% GST Because It Was Not Recognised as Puja Samagri appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Medical Negligence Case | Supreme Court Discharges Anaesthetist Who Prescribed Medicine Over Phone To Staff Nurse
articles.summary: The Supreme Court discharged an anaesthetist who had prescribed medication over the phone to a staff nurse, leading to a patient's death due to improper application of the medicine. The Court ruled that the anaesthetist could not be held guilty under Section 304-A of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) for the post-surgery complication, as the error occurred beyond her control. The case highlights issues of medical accountability and the need for clear protocols in healthcare delivery.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Monday (May 25) discharged an anaesthetist for an alleged post-surgery complication to the patient because of the medication prescribed by her over the phone to the nurse, which resulted in the death of the patient. articles.title: Ganga Expressway to cut freight time, unlock ₹30,000 cr annual savings on logistics
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government is developing the Ganga Expressway, a six-lane expressway (expandable to eight lanes) connecting Meerut to Prayagraj through 12 districts. It is designed as a major logistics and industrial corridor linking western and eastern Uttar Pradesh. The expressway is part of state-led infrastructure development to boost economic growth and industrial activity.

This ruling impacts how marital dispute cases are handled legally, particularly in cases involving dowry harassment or domestic violence.

articles.title: First Taiwan, then South Korea? How global AI supercycle is demoting Indian stock market
articles.summary: A global AI-led market shift is driving massive foreign capital outflows from India toward East Asia’s technology hubs, with overseas investors pulling nearly $24 billion from Indian equities this year. Fueled by the AI boom and a sharp rally in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company, Taiwan has overtaken India as the world’s fifth-largest stock market, while South Korea is emerging as another strong challenger.

articles.title: ​How TSMC and AI mania helped Taiwan leapfrog India in global equity rankings
articles.summary: ​Taiwan has overtaken India to become the world’s fifth-largest stock market, driven by a massive rally in semiconductor giant TSMC amid the global artificial intelligence boom. Strong investor demand for AI-linked infrastructure and advanced chip manufacturing has reshaped global equity rankings and redirected capital towards technology-heavy markets.​

articles.title: Global Market: Chinese AI and chip stocks rally after Huawei signals major tech leap
articles.summary: Chinese semiconductor stocks surged after Huawei announced a potential breakthrough in 1.4nm chip technology using 'LogicFolding' by 2031. The development aims to reduce China's gap with global leaders in AI and chip manufacturing amid US restrictions. The technology's commercial viability remains uncertain, with tests expected in upcoming Kirin mobile chips. The announcement highlights China's push to strengthen its AI and semiconductor capabilities.
articles.raw_summary: Chinese semiconductor stocks surged in Hong Kong following Huawei's announcement of a potential 1.4nm chip breakthrough using "LogicFolding" technology by 2031. This development aims to narrow the gap with global leaders and bolster China's AI ambitions amid US restrictions. While promising, the technology's commercial viability remains to be seen, with a key test expected with upcoming Kirin mobile chips.

articles.title: LIC 1:1 bonus bonanza: Is today the last day to buy shares for free rewards?
articles.summary: LIC has set May 29 as the record date for its maiden 1:1 bonus issue, making May 27 the last day for investors to buy shares for eligibility. This corporate action will see the insurer issue one free share for every share held, capitalizing reserves. The move aims to enhance share liquidity and affordability for its over 21 lakh shareholders.

The move aims to enhance share liquidity and affordability for its over 21 lakh shareholders.

articles.title: Oil Price Today (May 26): Crude oil nears $100 as US strikes southern Iran. What are experts saying?
articles.summary: Oil prices surged after the US conducted military strikes in southern Iran, escalating tensions in the region. Iran has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil supplies, raising concerns about prolonged supply disruptions. Analysts warn that ongoing conflicts could further destabilize energy markets, which may have implications for India's fuel imports and economic stability.

Iran has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, impacting global energy flows. Discussions are ongoing for a potential agreement. Analysts warn of prolonged supply disruptions.

articles.title: Huawei proposes new path for chip development amid US sanctions
articles.summary: Huawei is working on a new chip design strategy called the 'Tau Scaling Law' to improve semiconductor performance by 2031. The company aims to bypass US sanctions that restrict access to advanced chip technology. Huawei has already produced hundreds of chips using this approach and plans to integrate it into its upcoming Kirin chips with a 'LogicFolding' architecture. This development could impact global semiconductor supply chains and India's own policy on domestic chip manufacturing.
articles.raw_summary: Huawei aims for advanced chip design by 2031. The company is developing a new 'Tau Scaling Law' to improve chip performance. This innovation could bypass US sanctions restricting access to cutting-edge semiconductor technology. Huawei's upcoming Kirin chips will feature a related 'LogicFolding' architecture. The company has already produced hundreds of chips based on this new principle.

articles.title: US, Iran Inch Toward Deal as Gaps Remain on Uranium, Sanctions
articles.summary: The US and Iran are nearing a potential agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz, according to senior US officials. However, President Donald Trump stated he would not rush into a deal. The negotiations involve discussions on uranium enrichment and sanctions relief. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical oil shipping route.
articles.raw_summary: The US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, senior US officials said Sunday, even as President Donald Trump insisted he would not “rush” into an agreement.

articles.title: Curbs on foreign trips, focus on public transport: Gujarat govt issues measures to save fuel, forex
articles.summary: The Gujarat government has issued 11 measures to reduce fuel consumption and conserve foreign exchange, including curbs on official foreign travel, promotion of virtual meetings, use of electric vehicles, and local procurement. The move follows the West Asia crisis and aims to optimize public transport and energy efficiency. The government also urged the use of PNG canteens and local sourcing to further these goals.
articles.raw_summary: Gujarat government issues 11 point curbs on official travel, fuel use and foreign exchange after West Asia crisis, urging virtual meetings, EVs, PNG canteens and local procurement.

articles.title: Deepfakes & Deception
articles.summary: Deepfakes—synthetic audio, video, and images that mimic real people—are becoming increasingly convincing, posing a threat to trust in what people see and hear. The technology challenges existing legal frameworks that rely on the authenticity of visual and auditory evidence. This raises concerns about misinformation and the need for regulatory measures to address the risks posed by deepfakes. The article underscores the urgency for policy responses to mitigate deception.
articles.raw_summary: Deepfakes are no longer a technological curiosity. Traditionally, the law has relied on the trustworthiness of what people hear and see, but deepfakes fundamentally challenge that assumption. Synthetic audio, video, and images can now imitate real people and real events with enough precision to mislead viewers before any verification is possible. That is what makes deepfakes more than a technological novelty. The emergence of deepfakes presents a distinct regulatory challenge, as the existing le

articles.title: AI Promises Efficiency but the Fashion Industry Needs a Political Reckoning
articles.summary: Fashion for Good's report outlines a vision for an AI-driven fashion supply chain by 2036, emphasizing technical feasibility and operational seriousness. However, it raises unaddressed concerns about labour shifts, legal responsibility, and regulatory gaps that could either drive progress or perpetuate existing systemic failures. The discussion underscores the need for policy frameworks to manage AI's societal and economic impacts in industries like fashion.
articles.raw_summary: Ten years is a long horizon in any industry, but in AI it may be a category error. Fashion for Good's mapping of a 2036 AI-native supply chain is technically grounded and operationally serious. The structural questions it leaves unanswered, around rebound effects, labour transition, legal accountability, and regulatory reach, are the ones that will determine whether the vision it describes produces genuine progress or faster versions of the same systemic failures.

articles.title: SC rules exxsol hexane a pure hydrocarbon, duty cut to 2.5%
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that exxsol hexane is a pure hydrocarbon, lowering its import customs duty from 10% to 2.5%. Previously, customs officials classified it as motor spirit, attracting the higher duty. The court upheld a tribunal's order favoring the lower duty, which will impact India's import-export status. This decision affects the cost of importing exxsol hexane into the country.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that exxsol hexane is a pure hydrocarbon. This decision means imported exxsol hexane will attract a lower customs duty of 2.5 percent. Previously, customs officials wanted it classified as motor spirit, which carried a 10 percent duty. The court upheld a tribunal's order favoring the lower duty. This ruling impacts the nation's import-export status.

articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV released an encyclical titled 'Magnifica Humanitas,' urging strong regulation of artificial intelligence (AI) to prioritize humanity's well-being over profit. It emphasizes human dignity and the common good as guiding principles for AI governance.

articles.title: Japan maintains trade surplus for third straight month as energy imports decline
articles.summary: Japan reported a trade surplus for the third consecutive month, driven by strong exports of AI-related products and reduced energy imports. The decline in energy imports is linked to disruptions in oil routes caused by the war in Iran, which has also fueled inflation and weakened the yen. This economic shift may influence India's trade strategies, energy policies, and diplomatic engagements with Japan and oil-producing regions.

articles.title: El Nino threat: Centre rolls out kharif contingency plan to shield crops
articles.summary: The Union Agriculture Ministry has directed state governments to prepare district-wise strategies for kharif crops in response to the El Nino threat. States are advised to promote short-duration, water-efficient crops like pulses, millets, and vegetables to mitigate weather risks. The move aims to safeguard agricultural output amid potential climate challenges during the kharif season.
articles.raw_summary: Agriculture ministry has directed states to prepare district-wise crop strategies, and promote short-duration crops such as pulses, certain millets and vegetables during the kharif season because they require less water, mature quickly and help mitigate the vagaries of weather, said officials.

The campaign is in response to allegations of a leaked NEET-UG 2026 examination paper. The party has lost access to all its social media accounts, according to Dipke.

articles.title: Nearly 45% of organisations cite AI skills as largest workforce constraint; 54% cite moderate to low urgency on AI investment: Report
articles.summary: A report indicates that nearly 45% of organizations in India cite a lack of AI skills as the biggest constraint in their workforce, despite 62% of the population being of working age. Additionally, 54% of organizations view AI investment as having moderate to low urgency. The findings suggest a gap between workforce demographics and the skills required for emerging technologies like AI.
articles.raw_summary: This critical talent shortage emerges even as 62% of its citizens are in the working-age population

articles.title: DSEL issues order to increase egg distribution coverage in Karnataka schools to 90%
articles.summary: The Department of School Education and Literacy (DSEL) has ordered Karnataka officials to expand egg distribution in schools to 90% coverage by September 2026. The directive also includes creating awareness among students about the benefits of egg consumption. The move aims to improve child nutrition under government school meal programs.
articles.raw_summary: The DSEL has instructed officials to create awareness among students about the benefits of egg consumption, and has ordered that the target of 80% egg distribution should be achieved by September 2026.

articles.title: Major fire at oil godown in Manali
articles.summary: A major fire broke out at an oil storage facility in Manali after oil cans inside the warehouse exploded, intensifying the blaze and hindering firefighting efforts. The incident highlights safety risks associated with oil storage and could prompt regulatory reviews or policy discussions on industrial safety and environmental safeguards.
articles.raw_summary: Officials said the fire intensified as oil cans stored inside the warehouse exploded, making it difficult for personnel to approach the site initially

articles.title: Madhu lynching case: Kerala HC enhances sentence of 12 accused to life imprisonment, acquits one
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court increased the sentences of 12 accused to life imprisonment in the Madhu lynching case, where a mentally challenged tribal youth was killed in 2018 after being accused of stealing rice. The court acquitted one accused. The victim's mother and the Kerala government had appealed for enhanced punishment. The ruling addresses a case involving a vulnerable group, highlighting civil liberties concerns.
articles.raw_summary: Madhu’s mother and the Kerala government had filed appeals seeking enhanced punishment for the accused. The mentally challenged tribal youth was killed following the allegation that he had stolen rice from a grocery shop in February 2018

articles.title: Oil PSUs report sharp rise in demand for diesel, petrol in Telangana
articles.summary: Oil Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) in Telangana have seen a significant increase in demand for diesel (up 12.6%) and petrol (up 9%) in the first three weeks of May. The surge is attributed to intensified agricultural and harvesting activities, as well as consumers shifting from other suppliers to PSU outlets due to competitive pricing. The article highlights a shift in consumer behavior influenced by pricing dynamics in the fuel sector.
articles.raw_summary: Diesel sales up by 12.6%, petrol by 9% in first three weeks of May amid intensified agricultural, harvesting activities, retail consumers of other suppliers increasingly shifting towards PSUs outlet due to competitive pricing

articles.title: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz, halt transit charges under proposed deal with US: Report
articles.summary: The US and Iran are discussing a proposed deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz—one of the world's most vital oil shipping lanes—30 days after an agreement to end hostilities in the Middle East. The plan includes halting transit charges, which could ease tensions in the region. This development is significant for India due to its heavy reliance on oil imports from the Gulf, potentially impacting energy prices and supply chains.
articles.raw_summary: The plan, under discussion between US and Iran, would reopen Hormuz 30 days after the two countries agree to a deal to end hostilities in the Middle East.

articles.title: Tata Electronics plans to start chip packaging at upcoming Assam unit
articles.summary: Tata Electronics, part of the Tata Group, is expanding its chip packaging operations by setting up a high-volume manufacturing unit in Jagiroad, Assam. The move follows small-scale shipments from its first facility in Karnataka and aims to meet growing demand in automotive and industrial sectors. The expansion is expected to enhance the company's global credibility and scalability in semiconductor manufacturing.
articles.raw_summary: While the Tata Group company has already initiated small-scale shipments from its first commercial OSAT facility in Vemagal, Karnataka, the strategic pivot to the greenfield high-volume manufacturing unit in Jagiroad, Assam is aimed at building trust among global clients about the company’s capabilities while ensuring immediate scalability to meet surging automotive and industrial demand.

The move highlights the government's focus on combating drug trafficking and strengthening enforcement agencies.

articles.title: ‘RSS worker, and proud of it,’ says lawyer, IAF ex-sergeant who filed contempt plea against Kejriwal
articles.summary: Advocate Ashok Chaitanya, a former IAF sergeant and RSS worker, filed a contempt plea against Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal after facing online trolling for publicly responding to Kejriwal's 'objectionable' posts. It also warns against turning constitutional proceedings into publicity stunts for digital campaigns.

articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced progress in negotiations with Iran that could lead to the complete opening of the Strait of Hormuz for commercial shipping. This development is significant because the Strait of Hormuz is a critical global oil transit chokepoint, and its unrestricted access could influence international energy markets and trade dynamics.
articles.raw_summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced significant progress in negotiations with Iran, potentially leading to the complete opening of the Strait of Hormuz for commercial shipping.

articles.title: No girl should give up education over lack of toilets or sanitary pads: SC tells Centre
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (Justices J B Pardiwala and R Mahadevan) directed the central government to ensure that girls are not compelled to leave school because of inadequate sanitation facilities like toilets or sanitary pads. The bench emphasized that schools must provide safe and hygienic conditions to prevent girls from being pushed into domestic responsibilities. This ruling addresses a critical barrier to girls' education and highlights the state's obligation to uphold their right to schooling.
articles.raw_summary: A Bench of Justices J B Pardiwala and R Mahadevan observed that girls should not be pushed into domestic responsibilities or forced to stay at home simply because schools fail to provide safe sanitation facilities.

articles.title: Indore ice cream factory shut down over ‘extremely unsanitary conditions’
articles.summary: The Food Safety Department in Indore shut down the Siddhivinayak Ice Cream Factory after a raid revealed 'extremely unsanitary conditions.' The action was taken following orders from Chief Minister Mohan Yadav to crack down on food adulteration. The closure highlights enforcement of food safety regulations under government policy.
articles.raw_summary: The Food Safety Department raided Siddhivinayak Ice Cream Factory in Indore, after receiving orders from Chief Minister Mohan Yadav to take strict action against food adulteration.

articles.title: Ixigo posts 8.5% rise in Q4 revenue, clocks profit of Rs 32 crore
articles.summary: Ixigo's parent, Le Travenues Technology, reported an 8.5% year-on-year increase in operating revenue to Rs 308 crore for the March quarter, with profit after tax soaring 91% to Rs 32 crore. This growth was driven by a multi-modal strategy and AI-driven cost reductions.

articles.title: Park Medi World plans 1,500-bed expansion across North India by FY28
articles.summary: Sanjay Sharma, Group CEO & Whole-time Director, Park Medi World, expects revenue to rise to around ₹2,080 crore in FY27, supported by higher bed capacity, better occupancy, and an increasing contribution from complex procedures.

articles.title: Economy robust, external factors putting pressure on 3 Fs: Finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that India's economy remains robust but is facing pressure due to external factors affecting three key fiscal indicators: fiscal deficit, fiscal space, and foreign exchange reserves. She emphasized that these pressures could impact government policy decisions moving forward. The remarks were made in the context of ongoing economic challenges and global uncertainties.

articles.title: Marco Rubio's trip to 'great partner' India signals US need to repair ties
articles.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is visiting India to strengthen a partnership strained by tariffs and renewed U.S. engagement with Pakistan and China. The trip aims to boost trade, energy, and defense cooperation, addressing concerns over trade disputes and India's reliance on Russian oil. Despite efforts to stabilize ties, a comprehensive trade agreement remains elusive.

articles.title: India to revamp IIP with new base year, wider coverage from June 1
articles.summary: India will launch an updated Index of Industrial Production on June 1. The new IIP will feature a 2022-23 base year and include sectors like minor minerals and waste management. It will offer more detailed tracking of industrial activity. This revamp aims to provide a clearer picture of India's industrial growth.

articles.title: Now CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke moves court, asks HC to restore blocked X account
articles.summary: The founder of Citizens for Justice and Peace (CJP), Abhijeet Dipke, has approached the High Court to restore the organization's X (formerly Twitter) account, which was blocked. The move highlights concerns over digital free speech and platform moderation policies in India. The CJP's account had gained significant traction as a trending online collective in the country. The court filing seeks judicial intervention to address the account's restriction.
articles.raw_summary: The move to restrict CJP’s X account came at a time when the online collective had emerged as one of the country’s fastest-growing online trends.

articles.title: LIC targets double-digit revenue growth in FY27 as focus shifts to margin expansion
articles.summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India reported strong growth across premium collections, APE and VNB in FY26, while management also expects shareholder-focused measures such as bonus shares and higher dividends to support valuation over time.
articles.raw_summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India reported strong growth across premium collections, APE and VNB in FY26, while management also expects shareholder-focused measures such as bonus shares and higher dividends to support valuation over time.

articles.title: Huawei touts new chipmaking technology to sidestep US restrictions
articles.summary: Chinese tech giant Huawei announced a new method for making semiconductors that could help it avoid US restrictions on advanced chipmaking equipment. The company has faced US sanctions over espionage concerns, which it denies. This development could impact global semiconductor supply chains and geopolitical tensions, including implications for India's technology and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Chinese tech giant Huawei said on Monday it had developed a new way of making semiconductors that could get around its US-enforced lack of access to the most advanced chipmaking equipment. Huawei has been at the centre of a geopolitical standoff in recent years after Washington warned its equipment could be used by the Chinese government for espionage, an allegation the firm denies.

articles.title: Bellatrix readying ultra-low orbit satellites
articles.summary: Bellatrix, a Bengaluru-based company, is developing ultra-low Earth orbit (VLEO) satellites with targeted launches in 2027 or 2028. This project aims to address challenges posed by extreme atmospheric drag in very low orbits, an area currently underexplored globally. The initiative could position India as a leader in advanced satellite operations, potentially influencing future space policy and regulatory frameworks.
articles.raw_summary: The Bengaluru-based company is targeting launches in 2027 or 2028 and is positioning the project as a potential breakthrough in very low Earth orbit (VLEO) satellite operations, an area that remains largely unexplored globally because of the extreme atmospheric drag at those altitudes.

articles.title: I4C warns of phishing scam targeting iPhone users via fake Apple support messages
articles.summary: The Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C) has warned iPhone users about a phishing scam where scammers impersonate Apple Support to steal Apple IDs and OTPs via malicious links. Victims risk losing access to their devices and accounts. The scam underscores the need for vigilance and the use of Apple's official 'Find Devices' service for recovery.
articles.raw_summary: Beware of a cunning phishing scam targeting iPhone users who have lost their devices. Scammers, posing as Apple Support, are sending fake messages with malicious links to steal your Apple ID and OTP. This allows them to access your account and remove your device. Always verify links and use Apple's official 'Find Devices' service for recovery.

articles.title: Yatra Online Q4 net profit declines 46% to Rs 8.20 crore
articles.summary: Yatra Online's net profit saw a 46% drop to Rs 8.20 crore in the March 2026 quarter, impacted by the West Asia conflict which disrupted international travel demand. Despite this, the company reported strong full-year performance with revenue less service cost growth of 24.5% and adjusted Ebitda growth of 37.5%.

articles.title: Sebi targets 'finfluencer' misconduct to protect retail investors, freezes accounts of perpetrators
articles.summary: According to the SEBI order, the operators bought small/mid-cap stocks, artificially inflated prices by sharing unsubstantiated bullish tips to followers, and then dumped their shares at a massive profit, leaving retail investors with heavy losses.

Technology is also being used to create jobs and address labor shortages, with AI skills becoming crucial for hiring.

articles.title: India's prized Alphonso mango crop ruined by weather
articles.summary: A sharp difference in day and nighttime temperatures in December and January hurt flowering and fruit setting, while hotter than usual weather in April and May, probably due to the El Nino weather phenomenon, ​then spoiled the fruits themselves, an official said.

articles.title: Big infra projects clock Rs 5.6 lakh crore cost overrun
articles.summary: Major infrastructure projects in India have seen significant cost overruns. Projects valued at ₹150 crore or more have collectively exceeded their original budgets by ₹5.6 lakh crore. The total revised cost for 1,981 projects now stands at ₹42.8 lakh crore. Despite these overruns, expenditure on these projects indicates steady implementation progress.

articles.title: India emerges as Sri Lanka's second-largest export destination
articles.summary: India is now Sri Lanka's second-largest export market, overtaking the United Kingdom. This shift occurred between January and April of this year. The United States remains the top destination for Sri Lankan exports. Sri Lanka's overall exports saw growth in April, driven by merchandise and services. Coconut products and IT services performed well.

articles.title: Adequate availability of fertilisers for upcoming Kharif sowing season: Govt
articles.summary: India is set for a robust Kharif season with ample fertilizer supplies. Domestic production and imports have ensured comfortable stock levels. Over 200 lakh tonne of fertilizers are currently available, exceeding 50 percent of the projected demand. This proactive measure guarantees sufficient soil nutrient availability for farmers as sowing begins in June.

articles.summary: A prolonged West Asia conflict poses a risk to India Inc's profitability. Some sectors like ceramics and airlines face severe stress. Other export-oriented sectors might benefit from a weaker rupee.

articles.title: No love in Tokyo for mangoes from India this season
articles.summary: Japan has suspended fresh mango imports from India due to concerns over fumigation and disinfection deficiencies identified during a March inspection. This halt affects popular Indian varieties, with shipments certified after March 25, 2026, being rejected. The suspension will continue until India demonstrates improved operational standards.

articles.title: Piyush Goyal to visit Canada with 150 business leaders to boost trade ties
articles.summary: India's Trade Minister Piyush Goyal will lead a delegation of 150 industry leaders to Canada from May 25 to May 27. The visit aims to boost trade and investment between the two nations. Discussions will focus on a free trade agreement covering energy, critical minerals, textiles, and leather. Cooperation in technology, food processing, and clean energy will also be explored.

articles.title: El Nino, Strait of Hormuz risks may fuel fresh global food inflation surge: Citi Research Report
articles.summary: Global food prices are expected to rise due to worsening El Nino conditions and potential disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, according to a Citi Research report. Higher energy and fertilizer costs are also contributing to the inflationary risks. These factors could impact India's food imports, energy security, and agricultural policies, potentially leading to supply shortages or price volatility. The report highlights the need for proactive measures to mitigate these risks.

Higher energy and fertiliser costs are also a concern.

articles.title: Water purifier maker Kent RO delays IPO as Mideast war dents sentiment
articles.summary: Kent RO Systems has postponed its stock market debut. The Indian water purifier maker cites global market volatility, worsened by the Iran conflict, as the reason. Rising costs for raw materials also impact operations. The company will reassess its IPO plans in at least a year. Despite challenges, Kent expects revenue growth driven by low water purifier adoption in India.

US military strikes in Iran, despite diplomatic efforts, worsened regional instability. The situation, combined with anticipated Federal Reserve rate hikes, pressured gold and other precious metals. The developments could have implications for India's energy security and economic policies.

articles.title: India’s tech sector grows with fewer freshers as AI reshapes hiring
articles.summary: The share of entry-level hiring in the sector has fallen to around 15% in 2025 from 28% in 2024 as companies focus more on AI, cloud, cybersecurity and automation-focused roles. Emerging technology roles account for nearly 52% of hiring demand and are expected to touch 60% by the end of 2026, signalling a broader shift away from campus-led pyramid hiring model, experts said.

Fundraising also reached a low for the year.

articles.title: 3 elephant calves die within 17 days in Chhattisgarh’s Dharamjaigarh
articles.summary: Eight elephant calves have died in the Dharamjaigarh forest division of Chhattisgarh over the past five months, primarily due to drowning or getting trapped in marshlands. The deaths highlight concerns about wildlife conservation and forest management in the region. The incident may prompt demands for policy action to protect endangered species and address habitat risks.
articles.raw_summary: Eight elephant calves have died in Dharamjaigarh forest division over the past five months due to drowning, getting trapped in marshland, etc

articles.title: 5,406 communal posts identified, 3,200 taken down after ethnic violence erupted: Manipur DGP
articles.summary: Manipur's Director General of Police reported that 5,406 communal posts were identified on social media after ethnic violence erupted in the state, with 3,200 of them taken down. The incident highlights concerns over online hate speech and its role in exacerbating communal tensions. The DGP's statement suggests a need for stricter monitoring and regulation of digital content to prevent further unrest.
articles.raw_summary: 5,406 communal posts identified, 3,200 taken down after ethnic violence erupted: Manipur DGP

articles.title: Scientifically Speaking: The evolving map of Cholera
articles.summary: Researchers in the mid-2000s discovered that during cholera outbreaks, viruses that infect cholera bacteria increase in number, potentially helping to end the outbreaks. The study highlights the complex interplay between pathogens and could inform public health strategies. The findings are relevant to India, which faces periodic cholera challenges, though no immediate policy action is discussed.
articles.raw_summary: In the mid-2000s, researchers showed that as cholera bacteria multiplied in waters during outbreaks, viruses that preyed on them rose in number too, apparently causing the outbreaks to end

articles.title: HC agrees to hear plea against Centre’s order to vacate Delhi Gymkhana Club
articles.summary: On May 22, the Centre directed the iconic Delhi Gymkhana Club to hand over its 27.3-acre premises at 2, Safdarjung Road to the Land and Development Office (L DO) by June 5

articles.title: CBI likely to take over Twisha Sharma death probe within a day, Supreme Court informed
articles.summary: The Supreme Court was informed that the Madhya Pradesh government has written to the Centre recommending a Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) probe into the death of Twisha Sharma. The court recorded this statement, indicating a potential transfer of the case to the CBI for investigation. This move follows demands for a central agency inquiry into the case.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court bench recorded the MP government's statement that it has already written to the Centre recommending a CBI probe into the Twisha Sharma case.

articles.title: ‘Because accused is ex-judge’: SC on why CBI should take over Twisha Sharma case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to take over the case involving Twisha Sharma, citing the accused's former judicial position. The court also urged media professionals to exercise restraint while reporting on the case. This decision highlights the judiciary's role in ensuring fair investigation and accountability in high-profile cases.
articles.raw_summary: The top court also urged media professionals to exercise restraint while reporting developments in the case.

articles.title: Huawei Says It Has Workaround to Match Leading Chips
articles.summary: Huawei, the Chinese technology company, has stated that it aims to match cutting-edge semiconductor chips produced by Intel by 2031. This claim is part of Huawei's strategy to compete in the global semiconductor market. The development is significant for India's technology and policy landscape, as it may impact India's efforts toward self-reliance in semiconductor manufacturing and technology innovation.
articles.raw_summary: The Chinese tech juggernaut says it can match cutting-edge Intel semiconductors by 2031.

articles.title: ‘Laughed at me, called me stupid’: Founder of AI data collection firm on Pronto CEO Anjali Sardana
articles.summary: Human Archive founder Rushil Agarwal criticized Pronto's CEO Anjali Sardana over allegations that the Bengaluru startup recorded customers inside their homes without consent. The incident has sparked concerns about data privacy and surveillance practices in India's tech industry. Agarwal claimed Sardana laughed at his concerns and called him stupid when he raised the issue earlier.
articles.raw_summary: Human Archive founder Rushil Agarwal has taken a swipe at Pronto's Anjali Sardana amid the row around the Bengaluru startup recording inside customers’ homes

articles.title: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif, army chief Asim Munir meet China's Xi in Beijing as Iran war looms
articles.summary: Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and army chief Asim Munir met China's President Xi Jinping in Beijing amid rising tensions between the US and Iran. Pakistan recently hosted direct talks between the US and Iran, though no lasting agreement was reached. The meetings highlight Pakistan's central role in regional diplomacy, which could impact India's strategic interests, including energy security and border stability.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistan has emerged as a central mediator between the US and Iran, hosting historic face-to-face talks last month that failed to yield a lasting agreement.

articles.title: The former Air Force pilot who was allegedly recruited to train China’s military
articles.summary: Gerald Eddie Brown, a former U.S. Air Force pilot, was allegedly recruited to train China’s military after being grounded in the U.S. The case highlights concerns about foreign military recruitment and its geopolitical implications. Brown’s involvement raises questions about international military cooperation and potential security risks. The incident may influence India’s defence policies and strategic assessments regarding China.
articles.raw_summary: Combat veteran Gerald Eddie Brown was grounded in the U.S.—and looking for a chance to fly again.

articles.title: LIC 1:1 bonus share explained: What 21 lakh shareholders need to know before buying on record date
articles.summary: LIC has set May 29 as the record date for its maiden 1:1 bonus share issue, rewarding shareholders with one free share for each held. This move, capitalizing reserves, aims to boost liquidity. Investors must purchase shares by May 27 to be eligible for this significant corporate action.
